beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee at home.
beats.reasoning: Viktor starts his Monday morning at home in Ballard with full energy. A quick 30-minute routine sets him up for the day.
Viktor heads to his waiter shift Downtown, taking surface streets due to Light Rail closure (9am–3pm). He arrives early to prep for the lunch rush and the 11am FIFA match crowd.

beats.activity: Work morning shift at restaurant, prep for lunch.; Lunch shift during FIFA match; catches match between table turns.
beats.reasoning: Viktor works his day job, setting tables and prepping for the lunch service. The USA–Wales FIFA match at 11am will draw crowds to the bar section, so he's positioned to handle the rush.

beats.activity: Lunch break; grab a meal Downtown.
beats.reasoning: Viktor takes a breather at Pike Place Market, eating a quick meal. The sunny weather and his good energy make a market visit appealing; he enjoys the neighborhood vibe.
Viktor returns to finish his day shift, working the afternoon service through early evening. Steady work income is a priority.

beats.activity: Commute to food bank in Beacon Hill.
beats.reasoning: Viktor heads to his regular Monday volunteer shift. Light Rail closure doesn't affect this route; surface streets and his knowledge of Seattle timing make it reliable.

beats.activity: Volunteer at food bank: sorting, stocking, packing.
beats.reasoning: Viktor spends his Monday evening volunteering, which aligns with his generous character and community values. The sunny weather and good mood make this a rewarding shift.
Viktor heads home after volunteering, wrapping up his active Monday. The 45-minute commute accounts for evening traffic and his pragmatic route-planning.

beats.activity: Dinner, vinyl collecting, relax at home.
beats.reasoning: Viktor unwinds with a meal, browses his vinyl collection, and relaxes after a full day of work and volunteering. This wind-down at home is essential before sleep.
Viktor sleeps a solid 7.5 hours, recovering from his busy Monday. The sleep beat spans midnight, ending at 06:00 on Tuesday as required.

diary: Woke up to perfect weather—one of those June mornings that makes you remember why people actually live here. Got to the restaurant early enough to catch the tail end of USA-Wales on the bar TV. The energy was different today; customers kept one eye on their eggs and the other on the screen. Grabbed Pike Place between shifts, picked up a sourdough that was still warm. The Light Rail closure meant a longer commute to Beacon Hill, but honestly? I needed the walk. Three hours at the food bank sorting donations felt like the realest work I did all day. There's something about stacking boxes of canned beans that makes waiting tables feel less... pointless. Got home with enough light left to spin a Coltrane record and just sit with it. Tired but okay.
thought_process: The World Cup buzz gave the restaurant this electric feel—I found myself more present with customers, cracking jokes that actually landed because everyone was already in a good mood. But honestly, that energy made the afternoon shift feel hollow. That's why I prioritized the food bank even though I was exhausted; I needed to do something that mattered, something where I wasn't performing. The weather helped too—sunshine changes your whole calculus. I could've blown off volunteering, grabbed a beer downtown, but I remembered why I signed up in the first place. Some days the two halves of my life feel like they're competing; today they felt balanced.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
